reasoning: KMB stock has shown strong negative momentum, dropping from ~$129 to ~$120 over the past two months with technical indicators generating bearish signals (e.g., a MACD death cross). The company's Q3 2025 earnings drop on October 30, right before the first resolution date. Despite analyst targets generally remaining above $130, estimates have been consistently cut due to currency headwinds (300-400 bps margin drag), rebounding input costs (pulp/paper), and anticipated US import tariffs adding $170M+ in costs. Coupled with significant insider selling, the short-term expectation leans pessimistic for the immediate post-earnings window (44%). Over medium to longer horizons (November 25 and January 24), the natural positive drift of equities will gradually counteract the short-term negative momentum, pulling the probability closer to 0.5 as required by the horizon uncertainty rule. It's also worth noting there will be a mechanical headwind for the January 24 date due to KMB's expected early-December ex-dividend date.
